sourceMovie.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 who respond to its science-fiction will find The Hunger Games (2012) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 fans keep landing on Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire draws from the same science-fiction well as The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2013 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story (2016) captures the science-fiction appeal of The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Rogue One: A Star Wars Story at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Divergent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Divergent (2014). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Insurgent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Insurgent (2015) is the kind of film The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Alita: Battle Angel shares The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1's gift for science-fiction — the 2019 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Running Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 compelling — the science-fiction, the thriller — shows up in The Running Man (1987) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Chaos Walking
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 is alive in Chaos Walking (2021), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1?
faq.answer: Try The Hunger Games, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, The Hunger Games: Catching Fire—each shares DNA with The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1?
faq.answer: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 (2014) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Hunger Games Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
